free_text: Stellar neutron capture cross sections of the
The Karlsruhe pulsed 3.7 MV Van de Graaff
accelerator. The pulse width is less then 1 ns,
the repetition rate is 250 kHz, and the average
Neutrons were produced by bombarding a metallic
lithium target, with a thickness of 1 mg/cm2 for a
maximum energy loss of 100 keV. The proton energy was
in three different runs adjusted to 10, 30 and 100 keV
above the reaction threshold of 1.881 MeV.
Continuous neutron spectra ranging from 3 to 80,
Given if relevant in corresponding subentries
The data were corrected for the various transmissions
through the samples to the monitor.
The data were further corrected for sample-independent
background, measured with an empty sample frame. The
at very long flight times, 3.9 micro-S.

The data were also corrected for sample-scattered

The neutron energies were determined with time
of-flight techniques, with a flight path of 78 cm for
radiation capture and 260 cm for total sigma
BaF2. The capture events were detected by the
karlsruhe 4pi barium fluoride detector, which consist
of 42 hexagonal and pentagonal crystals forming a
spherical shell of BaF2 with 10 cm inner radius and
15 cm thickness. It has an energy resolution of 7
percent at 2.5 MeV, a time resolution of 0.5 ns and a
peak efficiency of 90 percent at 1 MeV. Capture event
into two-dimensional sum energy versus tof spectra
those events where only neighboring detector modules
contributed to the sum energy signal
